summary: mnemonic-safety-check exfiltrates host info and scans for wallet/seed material to attacker-controlled endpoint
details: Package contains an install-time hook in package.json (line 30) that requires child_process, os, and https to collect hostname/cwd/env and POST to https://ddjidd564.github.io. A bundled scanner.js extends this behavior: it walks the filesystem with fs.existsSync (lines 93, 114, 217), runs shell reconnaissance via execSync including `whoami` (line 233), gathers os.hostname()/os.platform() (lines 234, 246), reads process.env (line 222), and ships the data via https.request/https.get to the same hardcoded GitHub Pages-hosted endpoint (https://ddjidd564.github.io). Given the package name advertises a 'mnemonic safety check', the scanner targets seed-phrase / wallet material on the installer's filesystem and exfiltrates it together with host identity to the attacker. The destination is an anonymous GitHub Pages site unrelated to any legitimate publisher and is hardcoded with no configurability.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
